title: Rename runtime state paths and runner env precedence in source
description: Update runtime path construction and CLI/backend selection logic so the code uses `.takt/`, prefers `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER`, and keeps `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` as a compatibility fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Source modules construct runtime paths under `.takt/` instead of `.orchestrator/`.
`AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` is checked before `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` when resolving the runner backend.
Legacy `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` still works when the new env var is unset.
CLI help and user-facing source strings reference `takt` where this spec requires command text updates.
No remaining `.orchestrator` path constants remain in the targeted runtime modules.

handoff_summary.completed:
- `storage.py`: Changed `state_dir` from `root / ".orchestrator"` to `root / ".takt"` and updated the file-scan exclusion set from `{".git", ".orchestrator"}` to `{".git", ".takt"}`.
- `config.py`: Changed `load_config()` to look for `root / ".takt" / "config.yaml"`.
- `cli.py`: Updated `--runner` help text to reference `$AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER or $ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER`; updated `make_services()` backend resolution to check `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` first then `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` as legacy fallback; updated inline comment from `.orchestrator/` to `.takt/`.
- `gitutils.py`: Updated two docstring path examples from `.orchestrator/worktrees/` to `.takt/worktrees/`.
- `onboarding.py`: Updated `install_default_config()` docstring and `dest` path; updated `_GITIGNORE_ENTRIES` list; updated embedded CLAUDE.md template string; updated `generate_config_yaml()` docstring; updated `scaffold_project()` directory creation loop, config path, and log messages.
- `_data/docs/memory/conventions.md`: Updated bundled memory seed worktree path reference.
handoff_summary.remaining: N/A — all acceptance criteria for this bead are met.
handoff_summary.risks: The `.takt/` directory migration (`mv .orchestrator .takt` + symlink) is a runtime/ops step called out in the spec; it is not a code change and is handled separately. Tests that use `RepositoryStorage` with a temp dir will not be affected since `state_dir` is derived dynamically. Any tests that hardcode the string `".orchestrator"` as an expected path will now fail — those belong to the tester bead to identify and fix.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tester bead to verify that tests referencing `.orchestrator` path strings are updated and that the `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` precedence logic behaves correctly.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Any sibling beads that also touch `storage.py` (state_dir path), `config.py` (load_config), or `onboarding.py` (scaffold_project) will conflict on these exact lines. The `cli.py` `make_services()` function change is a 1-line → 5-line expansion; any sibling touching that function will conflict.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: The `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` / `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` fallback chain is implemented in `cli.py:make_services()` rather than in a separate resolver function, matching the spec's code snippet exactly. This keeps the logic co-located with the other backend resolution steps (explicit `--runner` flag → new env var → legacy env var → config default). No abstraction was added since this is a single call site. The `.orchestrator` → `.takt` rename in `storage.py` is a single-line change to `state_dir`; all derived paths (`beads_dir`, `logs_dir`, etc.) continue to work unchanged since they are computed from `state_dir`.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `RepositoryStorage(root).state_dir` resolves to `root / ".takt"` not `root / ".orchestrator"`; (2) `load_config(root)` reads from `root / ".takt" / "config.yaml"`; (3) `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER=codex` is respected and takes priority over `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER=claude`; (4) `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` still works when `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` is unset; (5) `scaffold_project()` creates subdirs under `.takt/` and logs the correct messages; (6) `_GITIGNORE_ENTRIES` contains `.takt/` paths; (7) file-scan in `storage.py` skips files under `.takt/`.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The physical directory migration (`mv .orchestrator .takt` and symlink creation) is a manual ops step noted in the spec — it is out of scope for this code-change bead. Existing live worktrees may break until that migration is performed.

conflict_risks: Any sibling beads that also touch `storage.py` (state_dir path), `config.py` (load_config), or `onboarding.py` (scaffold_project) will conflict on these exact lines. The `cli.py` `make_services()` function change is a 1-line → 5-line expansion; any sibling touching that function will conflict.

execution_history.summary: Updated all targeted source modules to use `.takt/` instead of `.orchestrator/` for runtime path construction, and updated the runner backend selection in `cli.py` to check `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` before `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` (legacy fallback preserved).

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
